title: Ocean Circulation Inverse Modeling
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Ocean Circulation Inverse Modeling: Ocean state estimation: infer 3D temperature, salinity, and velocity fields consistent with all observations (ECCO approach). The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.primitive.boussinesq_ocean…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~10000.0; forcing_uncertainty, mixing_parameterization_error set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: OGCM (MITgcm): primitive equations for T, S, u, v, w, eta; forcing from ERA5 winds and fluxes
E.world_state_x: global ocean T(x,y,z,t), S(x,y,z,t), u,v,w fields and surface forcing corrections
E.observation_y: Argo float T/S profiles, altimetry SSH, SST from satellites, hydrographic sections

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D_ocean_state is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 10000.0); forcing_uncertainty dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Observation gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: temperature RMSE < 0.3 K globally in ECCO v4. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~10000.0. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |forcing_uncertainty|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_iter * N_OGCM_calls) per optimization with N_OGCM = 20-year forward+adjoint per iteration
